questions.question: What is the main limitation of traditional RNNs?
keyPoints: Traditional RNNs can only capture information from the past

questions.question: What is a Bidirectional RNN?
keyPoints: A type of RNN that can capture information from both the past and the future

questions.question: What are the two main components of a Bidirectional RNN?
keyPoints: Two RNNs, one processing the input sequence in the forward direction and the other in the backward direction

questions.question: What is the purpose of the forward RNN in a Bidirectional RNN?
keyPoints: To capture information from the past

questions.question: What is the purpose of the backward RNN in a Bidirectional RNN?
keyPoints: To capture information from the future

questions.question: Explain the concept of Bidirectional RNNs and their applications.
keyPoints: Bidirectional RNNs can capture information from both the past and the future
Applications include language translation, text summarization, and sentiment analysis

questions.question: Compare and contrast Bidirectional RNNs with traditional RNNs.
keyPoints: Bidirectional RNNs can capture more contextual information than traditional RNNs
Traditional RNNs can only capture information from the past

questions.question: Explain how Bidirectional RNNs handle input sequences of varying lengths.
keyPoints: Bidirectional RNNs use padding or truncation to ensure that all input sequences have the same length
